title: Optional columns
summary: Learn how to insert data into tables with optional columns, using NULL and default values appropriately.

sketchBlocks.title: What are optional columns?
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, some table columns can be optional. This means you don't have to provide a value for them when inserting a new row. Optional columns are either defined with a DEFAULT value or allow NULLs. For example, in the `inventory_items` table, the `notes` column is optional because it allows NULL, and the `status`, `is_test`, and `last_updated` columns have default values.

```sql
CREATE TABLE inventory_items (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  category TEXT NOT NULL,
  price REAL NOT NULL,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
  is_test INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  notes TEXT NULL,
  last_updated TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '2026-02-01'
);
```

sketchBlocks.title: Inserting without optional columns
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When you insert a new row and omit optional columns, SQL will use the column's default value (if defined) or NULL (if allowed). For example, if you don't specify `notes` or `status`, the database will insert NULL for `notes` and 'draft' for `status`:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price)
VALUES (7, 'Keychain', 'Accessories', 3.50);
```
This will result in:
- `status` = 'draft'
- `is_test` = 0
- `notes` = NULL
- `last_updated` = '2026-02-01'

sketchBlocks.title: Inserting NULL explicitly
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
You can also explicitly insert NULL into a column that allows it. For example, to insert a new item with no notes:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, notes)
VALUES (8, 'Sample Coaster', 'Kitchen', 2.99, NULL);
```
This sets `notes` to NULL for this row.

quizDraft.title: Insert a row omitting optional columns
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new inventory item with id 9, name 'Desk Mat', category 'Accessories', and price 12.50. Omit all optional columns so that defaults or NULLs are used where appropriate.
quizDraft.hint: List only the required columns in your INSERT statement. The database will fill in defaults or NULLs for the rest.

help.concept: When you omit optional columns in an INSERT statement, SQL uses the column's default value or NULL if allowed.
help.hint_1: Only specify columns that are NOT NULL and have no default.
help.hint_2: The columns 'status', 'is_test', 'notes', and 'last_updated' will be filled automatically.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price)
VALUES (9, 'Desk Mat', 'Accessories', 12.50);

quizDraft.title: Insert a row with NULL for notes
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new inventory item with id 10, name 'Travel Mug', category 'Kitchen', price 18.00, and explicitly set notes to NULL. Omit other optional columns.
quizDraft.hint: Include the 'notes' column in your column list and use NULL as its value.

help.concept: You can explicitly insert NULL into columns that allow it by listing the column and using NULL in the VALUES clause.
help.hint_1: Add 'notes' to your column list and use NULL for its value.
help.hint_2: You do not need to specify columns that have defaults unless you want to override them.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, notes)
VALUES (10, 'Travel Mug', 'Kitchen', 18.00, NULL);

quizDraft.title: Insert a row overriding a default value
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new inventory item with id 11, name 'Art Print', category 'Decor', price 30.00, and set status to 'active'. Omit other optional columns.
quizDraft.hint: Include the 'status' column in your column list to override its default value.

help.concept: To override a default value, specify the column and your desired value in the INSERT statement.
help.hint_1: Add 'status' to your column list and set it to 'active'.
help.hint_2: You can omit columns like 'notes' or 'is_test' if you want their defaults or NULLs.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status)
VALUES (11, 'Art Print', 'Decor', 30.00, 'active');

quizDraft.title: Which columns are optional in inventory_items?
quizDraft.prompt: Select ALL columns in the inventory_items table that are optional when inserting a new row (i.e., you can omit them and the insert will still succeed).
quizDraft.hint: Look for columns that allow NULL or have a DEFAULT value.

help.concept: Optional columns are those that either allow NULL or have a DEFAULT value, so you don't have to specify them when inserting.
help.hint_1: Check the table definition for DEFAULT values and NULL allowances.
help.hint_2: Columns like 'notes', 'status', 'is_test', and 'last_updated' are not required in every insert.

quizDraft.title: Default value for status
quizDraft.prompt: What value will the 'status' column have if you omit it when inserting a new row into inventory_items?
quizDraft.hint: Check the table definition for the default value of 'status'.

help.concept: If a column has a DEFAULT value, SQL will use it when you omit the column in an insert.
help.hint_1: Look for the DEFAULT keyword in the CREATE TABLE statement for 'status'.
help.hint_2: The default for 'status' is a string value set in the table definition.

quizDraft.template: If you omit 'status' in your INSERT, it will be set to: ____
